channel.title: Combat Design on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Combat Design on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Using Combat Skills Outside Of Combat
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about using combat skills outside of combat.

item.title: Using Non Combat Skills In Combat
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about using non-combat skills in combat.

item.title: Action Points, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about action points, part two.

item.title: Real-Time Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about realtime Fallout.

item.title: Action Points
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about action points.

item.title: Why I Wanted Luck In Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I wanted luck in Fallout.

item.title: Damage Types
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about damage types.

item.title: Localized Damage Systems
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about localized damage systems.

item.title: Violence As The Default In AAA RPGs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about violence as the as the default in AAA RPGs.

item.title: Enemy AI
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about enemy Ai, and this is to answer a question from driver 3899, who asked: any chance you could talk a little bit about how you approach thinking about enemy AI?

item.title: Damage Numbers, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about damaged numbers again, because I already did a video on damage numbers, but that video was mostly about how they should appear in the HUD.

item.title: Damage Numbers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about damage numbers — yes, those little numbers that fly off of people when you hit them in games — because that’s actually an interesting topic to talk about, and

item.title: Enemy Progression
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about enemy progression.

item.title: The Origin of VATS
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the origin of Vats in Fallout and I’ll start by saying we didn’t call it bats in Fallout 1 and two.

item.title: Bosses
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about bosses and not me, even though I’m a boss.

item.title: Combat Coding
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about combat, like technical how to code combat kind of thing, like I’m going to talk data structures and event handling.

item.title: Arcanum Combat: Real Time vs Turn Based
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about arcanums, various combat modes, the real time, turn-based and fast turn based, kind of what the ideas were behind that, why it’s even in the game, um, what I wish

item.title: Arcanum Criticals
item.description: Hi everyone, this is Tim. Today I want to talk about how criticals work in Arcanum.

item.title: Fallout Criticals
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to explain exactly how criticals work in Fallout, and by criticals I mean both critical successes and critical failures.
